prompt: Wide architectural three-quarter view of a half-finished Massachusetts bathroom remodel, mid-project. Walls have green-tinted moisture-resistant cement board fastened to studs, mud rings for sconce boxes visible. Plumbing rough-in for a shower valve and a tub spout protrude from the cement board, copper stub-outs for the vanity. The floor is bare concrete subfloor with chalk-line marks. A folded blue painter's tarp at the doorway. A 5-gallon bucket of thinset, a notched tile trowel, and a wet saw set on the floor. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, dusty cool late-afternoon light filters in through a single bare window — beams of light catch suspended dust particles. Slightly desaturated palette, a red toolbox open in the foreground as the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
